name: Blake's Loan & Jewelry
description_short: Blake's Loan & Jewelry is a Compton-based pawn shop offering collateral-based cash loans and buying services for jewelry, electronics, and tools with 50+ years in business.
description_long:
Blake's Loan & Jewelry has operated in Compton, California for over 50 years, establishing itself as a local pawn shop serving the community's immediate cash and lending needs. The business is located at 407 W Compton Blvd near the intersection of W Compton Blvd and N Oleander Ave, and operates Monday through Saturday with extended hours (9 AM–6 PM weekdays, 9 AM–5 PM Saturday). They maintain a 4.0-star rating on Yelp based on 11 reviews, with 8 five-star and 2 one-star ratings indicating mixed customer experiences.

The company offers cash loans secured by collateral across a wide variety of items, with a stated commitment to paying "top dollar" for gold and other valuables. Their inventory includes jewelry, electronics, power tools, and other items of value. Customers can either pawn items temporarily (borrowing against collateral with the option to reclaim) or sell items outright for immediate cash. The business advertises accessibility as an amenity and provides phone-based inquiries at (323) 774-0940.

Blake's differentiates itself through staff reputation for fair valuations and quick transaction speed. Multiple positive reviews specifically mention staff members Joe and Cori for delivering friendly service and competitive payouts. One customer noted receiving "a little more" than expected and praised the speed of the lending process during financial hardship. The "top dollar" messaging suggests competitive pricing relative to other pawn shops in the area.

However, the business faces quality control concerns reflected in negative reviews. One customer reported purchasing jewelry with a defective rose gold band that separated within one month, raising questions about merchandise quality screening. Another review hints at past customer disputes. The pawn shop model inherently involves risk for borrowers: if loans aren't repaid, collateral is forfeited, and customers may overpay relative to actual item value. This category of lender typically charges higher effective interest rates than banks or credit unions and should only be used for immediate, short-term cash needs.
pros: Over 50 years operating in Compton with established local presence and community reputation
Staff members (Joe and Cori) specifically praised in recent reviews for fair valuations and efficiency
Quick transaction speed—customers report loans processed and funded rapidly during financial emergencies
Accepts wide variety of collateral (jewelry, electronics, power tools, and other items of value)
4.0 Yelp rating with 73% five-star reviews indicating generally positive customer satisfaction
Flexible lending approach—customers report receiving fair-to-generous payouts for items
Accessible location with convenient hours (9 AM–6 PM most days)
cons: Quality control issues documented—one customer received jewelry with defective rose gold band that failed within one month
Mixed reviews suggest inconsistent customer experiences; 18% negative (2 one-star reviews) indicate serious service failures
Collateral-based lending model means borrowers lose property if unable to repay loans within agreed timeframe
Pawn shop model typically carries higher effective interest rates compared to bank loans or credit unions
Limited online presence and transaction transparency—no website details on loan terms, interest rates, or redemption timelines
best_for: Individuals needing immediate cash against owned valuables (jewelry, electronics, tools) who can repay within a short timeframe
People with damaged or no credit seeking collateral-based lending outside traditional banking systems
Sellers of used electronics, power tools, or jewelry seeking quick cash without marketplace friction
diagnosis: Blake's Loan & Jewelry is best suited for borrowers in immediate financial crisis who own jewelry, electronics, or tools they can temporarily surrender. The primary caveat is that pawn lending carries risk of permanent asset loss if loans aren't repaid, combined with higher effective interest rates than traditional credit products and documented quality control issues with merchandise.
services: Collateral-based cash loans on jewelry and valuables
Cash loans secured by electronics and power tools
Gold and precious metal buying and appraisal
